name: pygda

metadata.name: pygda
metadata.summary: A Python library for Graph Domain Adaptation

documentation:
![](docs/pygda_logo.png)
-----
[![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pygda?style=flat)](https://pypi.org/project/pygda/)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pygda/badge/?version=stable)](https://pygda.readthedocs.io/en/stable/?badge=stable)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
[![contributions welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/contributions-welcome-brightgreen.svg?style=flat)](https://github.com/pygda-team/pygda/issues)

PyGDA is a **Python library** for **Graph Domain Adaptation** built upon [PyTorch](https://pytorch.org/) and [PyG](https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) to easily train graph domain adaptation models in a [sklearn](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/) style. PyGDA includes **20+** graph domain adaptation models. See examples with PyGDA below!

**Graph Domain Adaptation Using PyGDA with 5 Lines of Code**
```
from pygda.models import A2GNN

# choose a graph domain adaptation model
model = A2GNN(in_dim=num_features, hid_dim=args.nhid, num_classes=num_classes, device=args.device)

# train the model
model.fit(source_data, target_data)

# evaluate the performance
logits, labels = model.predict(target_data)
```
**PyGDA is featured for:**
* **Consistent APIs and comprehensive documentation.**
* **Cover 20+ graph domain adaptation models.**
* **Scalable architecture that efficiently handles large graph datasets through mini-batching and sampling techniques.**
* **Seamlessly integrated data processing with PyG, ensuring full compatibility with PyG data structures.**

## :loudspeaker: What's New?
**[12/2024]**. We now support source-free setting of graph domain adaptation.
- 3 recent models including `GTrans`, `SOGA` and `GraphCTA` are supported.

**[08/2024]**. We support graph-level domain adaptation task.
- 7 models including `A2GNN`, `AdaGCN`, `CWGCN`, `DANE`, `GRADE`, `SAGDA`, `UDAGCN` are supported.
- Various TUDatasets are supported including `FRANKENSTEIN`, `Mutagenicity` and `PROTEINS`.
- To perform a graph-level domain adaptation task, only one parameter is added to the model as follows:
```
model = A2GNN(in_dim=num_features, hid_dim=args.nhid, num_classes=num_classes, mode='graph', device=args.device)
```

## Installation
Note: PyGDA depends on [PyTorch](https://pytorch.org/), [PyG](https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/latest/), [PyTorch Sparse](https://github.com/rusty1s/pytorch_sparse) and [Pytorch Scatter](https://github.com/rusty1s/pytorch_scatter). PyGDA does not automatically install these libraries for you. Please install them separately in order to run PyGDA successfully.

**Required Dependencies:**

* torch>=1.13.1
* torch_geometric>=2.4.0
* torch_sparse>=0.6.15
* torch_scatter>=2.1.0
* python3
* scipy
* sklearn
* numpy
* cvxpy
* tqdm

**Installing with pip:**
```
pip install pygda
```

or 

**Installation for local development:**
```
git clone https://github.com/pygda-team/pygda
cd pygda
pip install -e .
```

## Quick Start

### Step 1: Load Data
```
from pygda.datasets import CitationDataset

source_dataset = CitationDataset(path, args.source)
target_dataset = CitationDataset(path, args.target)
```

### Step 2: Build Model
```
from pygda.models import A2GNN

model = A2GNN(in_dim=num_features, hid_dim=args.nhid, num_classes=num_classes, device=args.device)
```

### Step 3: Fit Model
```
model.fit(source_data, target_data)
```

### Step 4: Evaluation
```
from pygda.metrics import eval_micro_f1, eval_macro_f1

logits, labels = model.predict(target_data)
preds = logits.argmax(dim=1)
mi_f1 = eval_micro_f1(labels, preds)
ma_f1 = eval_macro_f1(labels, preds)
```

## Create your own GDA model
In addition to the easy application of existing GDA models, PyGDA makes it simple to implement custom models.
* the customed model should inherit ``BaseGDA`` class.
* implement your ``fit()``, ``forward_model()``, and ``predict()`` functions.

## Reference

| **ID** | **Paper** | **Method** | **Venue** |
|--------|---------|:----------:|:--------------:|
| 01      | [Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks](https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.02907)      |    Vanilla GCN     |   ICLR 2017    |
| 02      | [DANE: Domain Adaptive Network Embedding](https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2019/606)      |    DANE     |   IJCAI 2019    |
| 03      | [Adversarial Deep Network Embedding for Cross-network Node Classification](https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.07366) |    ACDNE     |   AAAI 2020    |
| 04      | [Unsupervised Domain Adaptive Graph Convolutional Networks](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3366423.3380219)  |   UDAGCN   |    WWW 2020    |
| 05      | [Adversarial Separation Network for Cross-Network Node Classification](https://dl.acm.org/doi/abs/10.1145/3459637.3482228)  |    ASN    |  CIKM 2021  |
| 06      | [Graph Transfer Learning via Adversarial Domain Adaptation with Graph Convolution](https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.01541)  |    AdaGCN    | TKDE 2022 |
| 07      | [Non-IID Transfer Learning on Graphs](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/26231)  |  GRADE   |   AAAI 2023    |
| 08      | [Graph Domain Adaptation via Theory-Grounded Spectral Regularization](https://openreview.net/forum?id=OysfLgrk8mk)  |   SpecReg    |   ICLR 2023    |
| 09      | [Structural Re-weighting Improves Graph Domain Adaptation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.03221) |   StruRW    |    ICML 2023    |
| 10      | [Improving Graph Domain Adaptation with Network Hierarchy](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3583780.3614928)  | JHGDA |  CIKM 2023  |
| 11     | [Bridged-GNN: Knowledge Bridge Learning for Effective Knowledge Transfer](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3583780.3614796)  |    KBL     |    CIKM 2023    |
| 12     | [Domain-adaptive Message Passing Graph Neural Network](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0893608023002253)  |   DMGNN    |    NN 2023    |
| 13     | [Correntropy-Induced Wasserstein GCN: Learning Graph Embedding via Domain Adaptation](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10179964)  |   CWGCN    |    TIP 2023    |
| 14     | [SA-GDA: Spectral Augmentation for Graph Domain Adaptation](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3581783.3612264)  |  SAGDA   |    MM 2023    |
| 15     | [Graph Domain Adaptation: A Generative View](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3631712)  |   DGDA   |    TKDD 2024    |
| 16     | [Rethinking Propagation for Unsupervised Graph Domain Adaptation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.05660)      |    A2GNN    |   AAAI 2024    |   
| 17     | [Pairwise Alignment Improves Graph Domain Adaptation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.01092)      |   PairAlign   |   ICML 2024    |
| 18     | [Empowering Graph Representation Learning with Test-Time Graph Transformation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.03561)      |   GTrans   |   ICLR 2023    |
| 19     | [Source Free Unsupervised Graph Domain Adaptation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00955)      |   SOGA   |   WSDM 2024    |
| 20     | [Collaborate to Adapt: Source-Free Graph Domain Adaptation via Bi-directional Adaptation](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3589334.3645507)      |   GraphCTA   |   WWW 2024    |


## Cite

If you compare with, build on, or use aspects of PyGDA, please consider citing "[Revisiting, Benchmarking and Understanding Unsupervised Graph Domain Adaptation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11052)":

```
@inproceedings{liu2024revisiting,
title={Revisiting, Benchmarking and Understanding Unsupervised Graph Domain Adaptation},
author={Meihan Liu and Zhen Zhang and Jiachen Tang and Jiajun Bu and Bingsheng He and Sheng Zhou},
booktitle={The Thirty-eight Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems Datasets and Benchmarks Track},
year={2024},
url={https://openreview.net/forum?id=ZsyFwzuDzD}
}
```

functions.name: pygda.metrics.metrics.eval_roc_auc
documentation:
ROC-AUC score for binary classification.

Parameters
----------
label : torch.Tensor
score : torch.Tensor

Returns
-------
roc_auc : float
    Average ROC-AUC score across different labels.

functions.name: pygda.metrics.metrics.eval_recall_at_k
documentation:
Recall score for top k instances.

# Parameters

label

: Labels in shape of `(N, )`.

score

: Scores in shape of `(N, )`.

k

: The number of instances to evaluate. `None` for
  recall. Default: `None`.

# Returns

recall_at_k

: Recall for top k instances.

functions.name: pygda.metrics.metrics.eval_average_precision
documentation:
Average precision score for binary classification.

# Parameters

label

: Labels in shape of `(N, )`, where 1 represents outliers,
  0 represents normal instances.

score

: Outlier scores in shape of `(N, )`.

# Returns

ap

: Average precision score.

functions.name: pygda.metrics.metrics.eval_micro_f1
documentation:
Micro-F1 score.

# Parameters

label

: Labels in shape of `(N, )`.

pred

: Predictions in shape of `(N, )`.

# Returns

f1

: Micro-F1 score.

functions.name: pygda.metrics.metrics.eval_macro_f1
documentation:
Macro-F1 score.

# Parameters

label

: Labels in shape of `(N, )`.

pred

: Predictions in shape of `(N, )`.

# Returns

f1

: Macro-F1 score.

classes.name: pygda.models.dgda.DGDA
documentation:
Graph Domain Adaptation: A Generative View (TKDD-24).

# Parameters

in_dim

: Input feature dimension.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

num_classes

: Total number of classes.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `3`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

weight_decay

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `0.0005`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

dec_dim

: Dimension of the graph decoder hidden layer. Default: `64`.

d_dim

: Dimension of the domain latent variables. Default: `64`.

y_dim

: Dimension of the semantic latent variables. Default: `256`.

m_dim

: Dimension of the random latent variables. Default: `128`.

recons_w

: Trade-off weight for reconstruction loss. Default: `1.0`.

beta

: Trade-off weight for kl loss. Default: `0.5`.

ent_w

: Trade-off weight for entropy loss. Default: `1.0`.

d_w

: Trade-off weight for domain loss. Default: `1.0`.

y_w

: Trade-off weight for cross entropy loss. Defalut: `1.0`.

m_w

: Trade-off weight for manipulating reconstruction loss. Defalut: `0.1`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.001`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `400`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `0`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

classes.name: pygda.models.grade.GRADE
documentation:
Non-IID Transfer Learning on Graphs (AAAI-23).

# Parameters

in_dim

: Input feature dimension.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

num_classes

: Total number of classes.

mode

: Mode for node or graph level tasks. Default: `node`.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `2`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

weight_decay

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `0.01`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

disc

: Discriminator. Default: `JS`.

weight

: Loss trade-off parameter. Default: `0.01`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.001`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `100`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `0`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

classes.name: pygda.models.adagcn.AdaGCN
documentation:
Graph Transfer Learning via Adversarial Domain Adaptation with Graph Convolution (TKDE-22).

# Parameters

in_dim

: Input feature dimension.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

num_classes

: Total number of classes.

mode

: Mode for node or graph level tasks. Default: `node`.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `3`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

weight_decay

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `0.`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

gnn_type

: Use GCN or PPMIConv. Default: `gcn`.

adv_dim

: Hidden dimension of adversarial module. Default: `40`.

gp_weight

: Trade off parameter for gradient penalty. Default: `5`.

domain_weight

: Trade off parameter for domain loss. Default: `1`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.004`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `100`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `0`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

Adversarial Separation Network for Cross-Network Node Classification (CIKM-21).

# Parameters

in_dim

: Input feature dimension.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

hid_dim_vae

: Hidden dimension of vae model.

num_classes

: Total number of classes.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `3`.

step

: Propagation steps in PPMI matrix. Default: `3`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

weight_decay

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `5e-4`.

lambda_r

: Hyperparameter for reconstruction loss. Default: `1.`.

lambda_d

: Hyperparameter for domain loss. Default: `0.1.`.

lambda_f

: Hyperparameter for different loss. Default: `0.001`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

adv_dim

: Hidden dimension of adversarial module. Default: `10`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.03`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `200`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `0`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

classes.name: pygda.models.strurw.StruRW
documentation:
Structural Re-weighting Improves Graph Domain Adaptation (ICML-23).

# Parameters

in_dim

: Input feature dimension.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

num_classes

: Total number of classes.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `2`.

cls_dim

: Hidden dimension for classification layer. Default: `128`.

cls_layers

: Total number of cls layers in model. Default: `2`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

gnn

: GNN backbone. Default: `GS`.

pooling

: Aggregation in gnn. Default: `mean`.

bn

: Batch normalization or not. Default: `False`.

reweight

: Reweight the edge in source graph or not. Default: `True`.

pseudo

: Use pseudo labels in target graph or not. Default: `True`.

ew_start

: Starting epoch for edge reweighting. Default: `100`.

ew_freq

: Frequency for edge reweighting. Default: `20`.

lamb

: Trade-off parameter for edge reweight. Default: `0.8`.

mode

: The training mode of StruRW. Default: `erm`.

weight_decay

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `0.0001`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.05`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `100`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `0`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

classes.name: pygda.models.sagda.SAGDA
documentation:
SA-GDA: Spectral Augmentation for Graph Domain Adaptation (MM-23).

# Parameters

in_dim

: Input feature dimension.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

num_classes

: Total number of classes.

mode

: Mode for node or graph level tasks. Default: `node`.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `2`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

weight_decay

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `0.003`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

alpha

: Trade-off parameter for high pass filter. Default: `1.0`.

beta

: Trade-off parameter for low pass filter. Default: `1.0`.

ppmi

: Use PPMI matrix or not. Default: `True`.

adv_dim

: Hidden dimension of adversarial module. Default: `40`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.004`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `200`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `0`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `0.`.

n_emb

: Adversarial learning module hidden dimension. Default: `128`.

pair_weight

: Trade-off hyper-parameter for pairwise constraint. Default: `0.1`.

step: int, optional

: Propagation steps in PPMI matrix. Default: `3`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.004`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `200`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `100`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

classes.name: pygda.models.gtrans.GTrans
documentation:
Empowering Graph Representation Learning with Test-Time Graph Transformation (ICLR-23).

# Parameters

in_dim

: Input feature dimension.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

num_classes

: Total number of classes.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `3`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

gnn

: GNN backbone. Default: `gcn`.

loop_adj

: Loops for optimizing structure.
  Default: `1`.

loop_feat

: Loops for optimizing features.
  Default: `4`.

ratio

: Budget B for changing graph structure. Default: `0.1`.

margin

: Test time loss hyperparameter. Default: `-1`.

strategy

: Graph augmentation strategy. Default: `dropedge`.

make_undirected

: Transform into undirected graph. Default: `True`.

weight_decay

: Weight decay (L2 penalty). Default: `0.`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

lr

: Learning rate. Default: `0.004`.

epoch

: Maximum number of training epoch. Default: `200`.

device

: GPU or CPU. Default: `cuda:0`.

batch_size

: Minibatch size, 0 for full batch training. Default: `0`.

num_neigh

: Number of neighbors in sampling, -1 for all neighbors.
  Default: `-1`.

verbose

: Verbosity mode. Range in [0, 3]. Larger value for printing out
  more log information. Default: `2`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the model.

classes.name: pygda.nn.prop_gcn_conv.PropGCNConv
documentation:
Args:

: in_channels (int): Size of each input sample, or {obj}`-1` to derive

  : the size from the first input(s) to the forward method.

  out_channels (int): Size of each output sample.
  improved (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer computes

  > $\mathbf{\hat{A}}$ as $\mathbf{A} + 2\mathbf{I}$.
  > (default: {obj}`False`)

  cached (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer will cache

  : the computation of $\mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{\hat{A}}
    \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2}$ on first execution, and will use the
    cached version for further executions.
    This parameter should only be set to {obj}`True` in transductive
    learning scenarios. (default: {obj}`False`)

  add_self_loops (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`False`, will not add

  : self-loops to the input graph. (default: {obj}`True`)

  normalize (bool, optional): Whether to add self-loops and compute

  : symmetric normalization coefficients on the fly.
    (default: {obj}`True`)

  bias (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`False`, the layer will not learn

  : an additive bias. (default: {obj}`True`)

  kwargs (optional): Additional arguments of

  : {class}`torch_geometric.nn.conv.MessagePassing`.

classes.name: pygda.nn.cached_gcn_conv.CachedGCNConv
documentation:
The graph convolutional operator from the ["Semi-supervised
Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.02907) paper

$$
\mathbf{X}^{\prime} = \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{\hat{A}} \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{X} \mathbf{\Theta},
$$

where $\mathbf{\hat{A}} = \mathbf{A} + \mathbf{I}$ denotes the
adjacency matrix with inserted self-loops and
$\hat{D}_{ii} = \sum_{j=0} \hat{A}_{ij}$ its diagonal degree matrix.

Args:

: in_channels (int): Size of each input sample.
  out_channels (int): Size of each output sample.
  improved (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer computes

  > $\mathbf{\hat{A}}$ as $\mathbf{A} + 2\mathbf{I}$.
  > (default: {obj}`False`)

  cached (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer will cache

  : the computation of $\mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{\hat{A}}
    \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2}$ on first execution, and will use the
    cached version for further executions.
    This parameter should only be set to {obj}`True` in transductive
    learning scenarios. (default: {obj}`False`)

  bias (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`False`, the layer will not learn

  : an additive bias. (default: {obj}`True`)

  kwargs (optional): Additional arguments of

  : {class}`torch_geometric.nn.conv.MessagePassing`.

hid_dim

: Hidden dimension of model.

num_classes

: Number of classes.

num_layers

: Total number of layers in model. Default: `4`.

dropout

: Dropout rate. Default: `0.`.

act

: Activation function if not None.
  Default: `torch.nn.functional.relu`.

disc

: Discriminator. Default: `JS`.

mode

: Mode for node or graph level tasks. Default: `node`.

kwargs

: Other parameters for the backbone.

classes.name: pygda.nn.mixup_gcnconv.MixUpGCNConv
documentation:
The graph convolutional operator from the ["Mixup for Node and Graph Classification"](https://dl.acm.org/doi/abs/10.1145/3442381.3449796) paper and ["Semi-supervised
Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.02907) paper
.. math:

```
\mathbf{X}^{\prime} = \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{\hat{A}}
\mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{X} \mathbf{\Theta},
```

where $\mathbf{\hat{A}} = \mathbf{A} + \mathbf{I}$ denotes the
adjacency matrix with inserted self-loops and
$\hat{D}_{ii} = \sum_{j=0} \hat{A}_{ij}$ its diagonal degree matrix.
The adjacency matrix can include other values than {obj}`1` representing
edge weights via the optional {obj}`edge_weight` tensor.
Its node-wise formulation is given by:
.. math:

```
\mathbf{x}^{\prime}_i = \mathbf{\Theta}^{\top} \sum_{j \in
\mathcal{N}(v) \cup \{ i \}} \frac{e_{j,i}}{\sqrt{\hat{d}_j
\hat{d}_i}} \mathbf{x}_j
```

with $\hat{d}_i = 1 + \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} e_{j,i}$, where
$e_{j,i}$ denotes the edge weight from source node {obj}`j` to target
node {obj}`i` (default: {obj}`1.0`)
Args:

> in_channels (int): Size of each input sample, or {obj}`-1` to derive
>
> : the size from the first input(s) to the forward method.
>
> out_channels (int): Size of each output sample.
> improved (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer computes
>
> > $\mathbf{\hat{A}}$ as $\mathbf{A} + 2\mathbf{I}$.
> > (default: {obj}`False`)
>
> cached (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer will cache
>
> : the computation of $\mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{\hat{A}}
>   \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2}$ on first execution, and will use the
>   cached version for further executions.
>   This parameter should only be set to {obj}`True` in transductive
>   learning scenarios. (default: {obj}`False`)
>
> add_self_loops (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`False`, will not add
>
> : self-loops to the input graph. (default: {obj}`True`)
>
> normalize (bool, optional): Whether to add self-loops and compute
>
> : symmetric normalization coefficients on the fly.
>   (default: {obj}`True`)
>
> bias (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`False`, the layer will not learn
>
> : an additive bias. (default: {obj}`True`)
>
> kwargs (optional): Additional arguments of
>
> : {class}`torch_geometric.nn.conv.MessagePassing`.

Shapes:
: - **input:**
    node features $(|\mathcal{V}|, F_{in})$,
    edge indices $(2, |\mathcal{E}|)$,
    edge weights $(|\mathcal{E}|)$ *(optional)*
  - **output:** node features $(|\mathcal{V}|, F_{out})$

methods.name: pygda.nn.kbl_base.PairNorm.__init__
documentation:
mode:
    'None' : No normalization
    'PN'   : Original version
    'PN-SI'  : Scale-Individually version
    'PN-SCS' : Scale-and-Center-Simultaneously version
    ('SCS'-mode is not in the paper but we found it works well in practice, especially for GCN and GAT.)
    PairNorm is typically used after each graph convolution operation.

methods.name: pygda.nn.kbl_base.PairEnumerator.pair_enumeration
documentation:
input:  [B,D]
return: [B*B,D]
input  [[a],
        [b]]
return [[a,a],
        [b,a],
        [a,b],
        [b,b]]

methods.name: pygda.nn.kbl_base.BridgedGraph.pair_enumeration
documentation:
input:  [B,D]
return: [B*B,D]
input  [[a],
        [b]]
return [[a,a],
        [b,a],
        [a,b],
        [b,b]]

classes.name: pygda.nn.reweight_gnn.GCN_reweight
documentation:
The graph convolutional operator from the ["Semi-supervised
Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks"](https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.02907) paper

$$
\mathbf{X}^{\prime} = \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{\hat{A}} \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{X} \mathbf{\Theta},
$$

where $\mathbf{\hat{A}} = \mathbf{A} + \mathbf{I}$ denotes the
adjacency matrix with inserted self-loops and
$\hat{D}_{ii} = \sum_{j=0} \hat{A}_{ij}$ its diagonal degree matrix.
The adjacency matrix can include other values than {obj}`1` representing
edge weights via the optional {obj}`edge_weight` tensor.

Its node-wise formulation is given by:

$$
\mathbf{x}^{\prime}_i = \mathbf{\Theta}^{\top} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(v) \cup \{ i \}} \frac{e_{j,i}}{\sqrt{\hat{d}_j \hat{d}_i}} \mathbf{x}_j
$$

with $\hat{d}_i = 1 + \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} e_{j,i}$, where
$e_{j,i}$ denotes the edge weight from source node {obj}`j` to target
node {obj}`i` (default: {obj}`1.0`)

Args:

: in_channels (int): Size of each input sample, or {obj}`-1` to derive

  : the size from the first input(s) to the forward method.

  out_channels (int): Size of each output sample.
  improved (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer computes

  > $\mathbf{\hat{A}}$ as $\mathbf{A} + 2\mathbf{I}$.
  > (default: {obj}`False`)

  cached (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`True`, the layer will cache

  : the computation of $\mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2} \mathbf{\hat{A}}
    \mathbf{\hat{D}}^{-1/2}$ on first execution, and will use the
    cached version for further executions.
    This parameter should only be set to {obj}`True` in transductive
    learning scenarios. (default: {obj}`False`)

  add_self_loops (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`False`, will not add

  : self-loops to the input graph. (default: {obj}`True`)

  normalize (bool, optional): Whether to add self-loops and compute

  : symmetric normalization coefficients on the fly.
    (default: {obj}`True`)

  bias (bool, optional): If set to {obj}`False`, the layer will not learn

  : an additive bias. (default: {obj}`True`)

  kwargs (optional): Additional arguments of

  : {class}`torch_geometric.nn.conv.MessagePassing`.

Shapes:
: - **input:**
    node features $(|\mathcal{V}|, F_{in})$,
    edge indices $(2, |\mathcal{E}|)$,
    edge weights $(|\mathcal{E}|)$ *(optional)*
  - **output:** node features $(|\mathcal{V}|, F_{out})$
